summary: The hardiest of Enkianthus species is E. campanulatus ( furin-tsutsuji or redvein enkianthus ), a medium-sized, narrow, upright, deciduous shrub. Its bright green glossy foliage gives brilliant coppery to red fall colors. In spring it offers a profusion of bell-shaped ( c ampanula, "little bell"), creamy white flowers with red veins, similar to those of the distantly related Pieris.
